# Copilot Instructions — SAIFE Resource Hub

## Project Overview

SAIFE (Spotlight on Artificial Intelligence and Freedom of Expression) is a fully static,
pre-rendered informational website for the OSCE. It covers AI's impact on freedom of expression
and media freedom. Content is authored in Google Docs/Sheets and pulled into the repo as committed
JSON files. The site deploys to GitHub Pages and a dedicated SSH server.

---

## Project Stack

| Layer | Technology |
| --- | --- |
| Framework | SvelteKit 1.27 + Svelte 4.2 |
| Language | TypeScript 5.4 (strict mode) |
| Build tool | Vite 4.4 |
| Adapter | `@sveltejs/adapter-static` — fully prerendered, no SSR |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS 3.3 (JIT) + PostCSS |
| HTML parsing | `cheerio` (data pipeline only) |
| Google Drive | `googleapis` service account (data pipeline only) |
| Package manager | npm (`package-lock.json` is committed) |
| Runtime | Node.js 18 |

No database, no backend API, no auth system, no test framework.

---

## Build Commands

```bash
# Development server (localhost:5173)
npm run start

# Production build → ./build/
npm run compile

# Build only the SvelteKit side
npm run compile:svelte

# Pull data from Google Drive (requires .auth.json)
npm run data:google

# Lint everything (ESLint + svelte-check + tsc + Prettier)
npm run lint

# Auto-fix linting issues
npm run fix

# Type checking only
npm run lint:tsc

# Svelte component checking
npm run lint:svelte
```

The production build requires `BASE_PATH=/saife-toolkit NODE_ENV=production` — the CI workflow
sets these automatically. Local dev uses root-relative paths, production uses `/saife-toolkit`
as the base path.

---

## Coding Conventions

### TypeScript

- Strict mode is enabled (`"strict": true`). Never use `any` without justification.
- `checkJs: true` — JavaScript files are also type-checked.
- Do not add explicit return types to functions unless the linter requires it
  (`@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type` is off).
- Use `satisfies` for type-narrowing where appropriate (e.g., SvelteKit `load` functions).

### Formatting (Prettier)

- **No bracket spacing**: `{a: 1}` not `{ a: 1 }`
- **Trailing commas everywhere** (ES5+)
- **Arrow parens always**: `(x) => x` not `x => x`
- **Print width**: 100 characters
- **Indent**: 2 spaces
- Run `npm run fix` to auto-format before committing.

### ESLint

- Extends `airbnb-base`, `airbnb-typescript/base`, `@typescript-eslint/recommended`,
  `unicorn/recommended`, `svelte/recommended`.
- **Import order is enforced** (`simple-import-sort`). Imports will be auto-sorted by `npm run fix`.
- **Filenames must be kebab-case** (`unicorn/filename-case`).
- `console.log` is a warning — use `console.warn` or `console.error` for intentional output.
- Unused variables are errors unless prefixed with `_`.
- `import/prefer-default-export` is off — named exports are preferred.

### Svelte components

- One component per file, filename in kebab-case.
- Tailwind classes are used for all styling. Custom CSS in `src/app.css` is for global resets and
  Tailwind's `@layer` extensions only.
- Custom OSCE/SAIFE brand colors are defined in `tailwind.config.cjs` under
  `theme.extend.colors`. Use these (`blue-osce`, `blue-saife`, `green-saife`, etc.), not raw hex.
- Always import `base` from `"$app/paths"` when constructing internal links, e.g.:
  ```svelte
  import {base} from "$app/paths";
  <a href="{base}/resources">Resources</a>
  ```

### SvelteKit routing

- All routes are in `src/routes/` using the file-based convention (`+page.svelte`,
  `+page.ts`, `+layout.svelte`, `+layout.ts`).
- `export const prerender = true` is set in `src/routes/+layout.ts` and applies globally.
  Do not add `ssr = false` or server-only load functions — this is a fully static site.
- Data is loaded by importing JSON directly in `+page.ts` load functions and passing through
  `PageData`. Do not fetch at runtime.

---

## Rules for Minimal Diffs

- **Do not reformat files you are not changing.** Prettier formatting changes create noise. Only
  format files you have edited.
- **Do not reorganise imports** in files you are not otherwise changing. Import order is enforced
  by ESLint and will be corrected during `npm run fix`.
- **Do not change `build/` or `docs/`** manually. These directories contain committed build
  artifacts. They are regenerated by running `npm run compile` and must be recommitted as a
  unit with the source change that caused them.
- **Do not alter `package-lock.json`** unless you are explicitly adding or removing a dependency.
- **Keep changes scoped.** A UI fix in one component should not touch unrelated components,
  global CSS, or data files.
- **Prefer editing existing utilities** in `src/lib/utils.ts`, `src/lib/formatter.ts`, etc.
  over creating new files.

---

## Areas to Treat Carefully

### `src/lib/google.ts` — Google Drive data pipeline

This file handles all data ingestion. It requires a `.auth.json` Google service account key
at the project root (gitignored). Changing field names, folder IDs, or export formats here will
break the data pipeline. The root folder ID and glossary folder ID are hardcoded constants
(marked with `FIXME`); do not change them without verifying the Drive structure.

### `src/data/*.json` and `src/data/resources/` — Committed content data

These files are generated by `npm run data:google`. **Do not hand-edit them.** Changes will be
overwritten the next time the data pipeline runs. Image files in `src/data/resources/` are
downloaded by the pipeline.

### `build/` and `docs/` — Committed build artifacts

Both directories are committed to the repository. `docs/` is the GitHub Pages source.
**Never edit files in these directories directly.** Always rebuild with `npm run compile` and
commit the regenerated output alongside the source changes.

### `svelte.config.js` — Base path logic

The `base` variable switches between `""` (dev) and `"/saife-toolkit"` (production) based on
`NODE_ENV`. Breaking this will cause all internal links to 404 on GitHub Pages. If you add a new
route, verify it works with the base path applied.

### `src/lib/formatter.ts` — HTML post-processor

This file transforms raw Google Docs HTML export into clean HTML for rendering. It strips Google
redirect URLs, rewrites heading anchors to slugs, and converts inline styles. Changes here affect
all content rendered from Google Docs and should be tested against multiple resource entries.

### Tailwind configuration (`tailwind.config.cjs`)

Custom colour tokens and font configuration live here. Changing colour names or removing tokens
will silently produce unstyled output — Tailwind purges unused classes. Verify the full site
renders after any config change.

### `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — CI/CD

The deploy workflow uses the official GitHub Pages Actions (`upload-pages-artifact`,
`deploy-pages`). It passes `BASE_PATH=/saife-toolkit` and `NODE_ENV=production` to the build.
Do not remove these environment variables. The job uses `cancel-in-progress: true` concurrency —
only one deploy runs at a time.

---

## Validation Steps After Changes

Run these in order before committing:

```bash
# 1. Type-check TypeScript and Svelte components
npm run lint:tsc
npm run lint:svelte

# 2. Lint and format
npm run lint

# 3. Auto-fix any fixable issues
npm run fix

# 4. Full production build (verifies no build-time errors)
npm run compile

# 5. Preview the built site locally
npx serve build
```

For changes to the data pipeline (`src/lib/google.ts`, `src/indexctl.ts`):

```bash
# Requires .auth.json with a valid service account key
npm run data:google
```

For changes that affect routing, navigation, or the base path:

- Start `npm run start` and manually verify internal links navigate correctly.
- Run `npm run compile` and inspect the generated `build/` for the affected routes.

For UI changes:

- Check both light and dark backgrounds (pages use both `images/header-light.png` and
  `images/header-dark.png` variants).
- Verify the page renders correctly at mobile widths (the site uses responsive Tailwind classes).
